story.iWant: the tree to explode with a specific "radial force" and "damping" effect
story.soThat: it feels like a release of energy rather than just linear movement
story.tasks: - Shader Development - Create src/shaders/particle.vert implementing Quadratic Bezier interpolation - Create src/shaders/particle.frag handling basic color rendering with "Glow via Intensity" (boost color when uProgress is low) - Use NormalBlending (avoid additive blending to preserve photo visibility) - Component Update (TreeParticles.tsx) - Calculate positionStart (current tree positions) - Calculate positionEnd (random floating positions in a cloud) - Calculate controlPoint (Start + Explosion Vector) for the Bezier curve - Calculate aRandom attribute in JS to avoid expensive GLSL noise - Calculate aUvOffset with sufficient randomization to avoid tiling patterns - Pass these as attributes to the ShaderMaterial - Pass uniforms: uProgress, uTime, uColor - Implement useFrame loop to interpolate uProgress based on isExploded state - Integration & Verification - Connect useStore isExploded state - Verify explosion animation smoothness (>30fps) - Verify "Implosion" (returning to tree) works by reversing uProgress

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Given the TreeParticles component, When I click the tree (triggering isExploded), Then the particles should move using the "GPU State Machine" pattern. 2. And the movement should follow a Bezier curve (Start -> Explosion Vector -> Float Position). 3. And the animation should be driven by a uProgress uniform (0 to 1). 4. And performance should remain >30fps during explosion. 5. And particle.vert and particle.frag shaders are implemented and used.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Visual Engine Upgrade
doc.snippet: This epic focuses on upgrading the core 3D experience... implementing custom GLSL shaders for high-performance particle animation, a "GPU State Machine" for smooth transitions.

doc.title: Architecture Decision Document
doc.snippet: GPU State Machine: Adopts the proposed vertex shader logic where uProgress drives the transition from positionStart (Tree) to positionEnd (Photo Cloud) via a Bezier curve.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Implement particle.vert shader with the Bezier logic. Use useFrame to interpolate uProgress based on isExploded state.

item.reason: Target component for shader integration. CURRENT STATE: Uses CPU-based animation (useFrame loop updating positions array) with multiple layers (Entity, Glow, Ornaments, Crown, Gifts). Implements 'British Theme' distribution and dynamic coloring based on useStore.
Source of isExploded state and theme configuration. CURRENT STATE: Fully implemented with persist middleware. Manages treeColor, particleCount, and isExploded state.
Source of color values for the shader.

constraints.constraint: Must use Custom GLSL Shaders (vertex/fragment) for performance.
Must use a single Draw Call (Points or InstancedMesh).
Must implement reversible animation (Implosion) via Time Reversal.
Must use NormalBlending and simulate glow via color intensity boost.
Must pre-calculate random values (aRandom) in JS, avoiding GLSL random functions.

interface.name: Shader Uniforms
Geometry Attributes
interface.signature: aPositionStart: vec3 aPositionEnd: vec3 aControlPoint: vec3 aColor: vec3

tests.standards: Use r3f-perf for performance monitoring. Visual verification of animation smoothness and correctness.

ideas.idea: Verify FPS stays above 30 during full explosion sequence.
Verify particles return to exact original positions on implosion.
